---
title: "scRNA Annotation Method Report: scGPT"
date: today
jupyter: python3
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    toc-expand: 3
    toc-location: left
    html-math-method: katex
    embed-resources: true
    page-layout: full
    html-table-processing: none
    theme: lumen
    fig-width: 10
    fig-height: 5
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
---

```{python}
from pathlib import Path
import sys

from IPython.display import Markdown, display
import pandas as pd
import plotly.express as px

LIB_DIR = Path(".").resolve() / "lib"
if str(LIB_DIR) not in sys.path:
    sys.path.insert(0, str(LIB_DIR))

from report_utils import format_table, load_report_context, package_versions_table, style_bar

ROOT = Path(".").resolve()
context = load_report_context(ROOT)
```

## Current Status

```{python}
display(
    Markdown(
        """
This is a **scaffold report** for a future Python-native **scGPT** backend.
The goal is to keep the report structure ready while treating foundation-model
annotation as an explicitly advanced, research-oriented option.
"""
    )
)
```

```{python}
status_df = pd.DataFrame(
    [
        {"field": "method", "value": "scgpt"},
        {"field": "implemented_in_runtime", "value": "no"},
        {"field": "intended_role", "value": "foundation-model-based annotation and mapping"},
        {"field": "selected_in_current_run", "value": "scgpt" in context["selected_methods"]},
    ]
)
format_table(status_df)
```

## Why This Backend Is Different

```{python}
fit_df = pd.DataFrame(
    [
        {"consideration": "foundation-model workflow", "impact": "more flexible but operationally heavier than standard classifiers"},
        {"consideration": "checkpoint management", "impact": "requires clear handling of pretrained or fine-tuned weights"},
        {"consideration": "compute cost", "impact": "GPU availability may strongly affect usability"},
    ]
)
format_table(fit_df)
```

```{python}
profile_df = pd.DataFrame(
    [
        {"dimension": "research flexibility", "score": 5},
        {"dimension": "routine operational simplicity", "score": 2},
        {"dimension": "checkpoint dependence", "score": 5},
        {"dimension": "compute demand", "score": 4},
        {"dimension": "future potential", "score": 5},
    ]
)
profile_plot = px.bar(
    profile_df,
    x="dimension",
    y="score",
    title="Expected scGPT profile in this template",
    range_y=[0, 5.5],
)
style_bar(profile_plot, height=420)
```

## Paper And Documentation Takeaways

The scGPT paper frames the method as a foundation-model approach for
single-cell multi-omic analysis, not just a narrow annotation utility. That
gives it interesting long-term potential, but also means the reporting and
runtime contract need to be clearer about checkpoint provenance, vocabulary
alignment, and compute cost than they would be for a simpler classifier.

```{python}
takeaways_df = pd.DataFrame(
    [
        {"takeaway": "foundation-model orientation", "implication": "annotation is only one possible downstream use case"},
        {"takeaway": "large pretrained checkpoints", "implication": "checkpoint provenance should be visible in reports"},
        {"takeaway": "gene vocabulary alignment", "implication": "input validation will need to be stricter than for CellTypist"},
        {"takeaway": "higher operational cost", "implication": "GPU and runtime summaries matter for reproducibility"},
    ]
)
format_table(takeaways_df)
```

## When scGPT Might Be Worth The Complexity

```{python}
fit_cases_df = pd.DataFrame(
    [
        {"situation": "research project evaluating frontier annotation methods", "recommendation": "strong candidate for an experimental backend"},
        {"situation": "need a routine facility-grade default", "recommendation": "prefer CellTypist or later scANVI support"},
        {"situation": "checkpoint provenance is unclear", "recommendation": "do not trust outputs until lineage is explicit"},
        {"situation": "GPU access is limited", "recommendation": "consider whether the operational cost is justified"},
    ]
)
format_table(fit_cases_df)
```

## Required Future Inputs

```{python}
planned_df = pd.DataFrame(
    [
        {"input": "model checkpoint", "purpose": "pretrained or fine-tuned weights"},
        {"input": "gene vocabulary mapping", "purpose": "align query genes to model vocabulary"},
        {"input": "fine-tuning controls", "purpose": "decide inference-only vs adaptation"},
        {"input": "GPU availability", "purpose": "practical runtime requirement for larger models"},
    ]
)
format_table(planned_df)
```

## Planned Report Contents

```{python}
diagnostics_df = pd.DataFrame(
    [
        {"planned_section": "predicted label summary", "content": "cell- and cluster-level annotation output"},
        {"planned_section": "uncertainty review", "content": "confidence or fallback uncertainty summaries"},
        {"planned_section": "cross-method agreement", "content": "compare scGPT to other backends"},
        {"planned_section": "runtime summary", "content": "document resource use and checkpoint provenance"},
    ]
)
format_table(diagnostics_df)
```

## Reporting Requirements Unique To scGPT

```{python}
requirements_df = pd.DataFrame(
    [
        {"requirement": "checkpoint provenance", "reason": "users need to know which pretrained or fine-tuned model generated the result"},
        {"requirement": "gene vocabulary mapping summary", "reason": "annotation quality depends on how well query genes map to the model vocabulary"},
        {"requirement": "runtime resource summary", "reason": "GPU and memory cost are part of reproducibility here"},
        {"requirement": "fallback uncertainty handling", "reason": "foundation-model outputs should not be overinterpreted when confidence is weak"},
    ]
)
format_table(requirements_df)
```

## Readiness Checklist

```{python}
checklist_df = pd.DataFrame(
    [
        {"task": "define checkpoint/config contract", "status": "pending"},
        {"task": "add vocabulary alignment checks", "status": "pending"},
        {"task": "write scGPT result tables", "status": "pending"},
        {"task": "add method-specific review figures", "status": "pending"},
    ]
)
format_table(checklist_df)
```

```{python}
checklist_plot = px.bar(
    checklist_df.assign(done=[0, 0, 0, 0]),
    x="task",
    y="done",
    title="scGPT implementation status",
)
style_bar(checklist_plot, height=420)
```

## References Consulted

```{python}
references_df = pd.DataFrame(
    [
        {
            "resource": "scGPT paper",
            "role": "method framing and foundation-model scope",
            "link": "https://www.nature.com/articles/s41592-024-02201-0",
        },
        {
            "resource": "scGPT documentation",
            "role": "package-level usage and implementation context",
            "link": "https://scgpt.readthedocs.io/",
        },
    ]
)
format_table(references_df)
```

## Session Info

```{python}
session_df = package_versions_table(["scanpy", "anndata", "pandas", "numpy", "plotly", "scgpt"])
format_table(session_df)
```
